title: Daily Market Digest: May 29, 2026
subtitle: Wall Street Extends Gains on Tech Strength and Geopolitical Optimism

items.headline: U.S. Stocks Continue Upward Trend, Set for Ninth Consecutive Winning Week
items.summary: U.S. equities are extending their gains on Friday, building on Thursday's all-time highs, driven by strong performance in technology and healthcare sectors. Overall market sentiment remains positive, fueled by robust corporate earnings, particularly in AI, and easing geopolitical tensions as reports suggest progress in U.S.-Iran ceasefire talks. These developments have also contributed to a decline in oil prices, further muting inflation concerns.

items.statContext: Up in morning trading, extending all-time highs.
Rose in morning trading, led by technology stocks.
Gained 240 points as of 10:29 a.m. ET.

items.headline: Dell Technologies Soars on AI Demand
items.summary: Shares surged after reporting record Q1 revenue of $43.8 billion, massive non-GAAP EPS of $4.86, and raising its full-year forecast for fiscal 2027, citing powerful demand for AI computing.

items.headline: Replimune Group Jumps on FDA Agreement
items.summary: The biotech firm's shares surged after announcing a formal agreement with the FDA on a path to resubmit its Biologics License Application (BLA) for its lead asset, RP1.

items.headline: NetApp Rallies on Strong Earnings
items.summary: The data storage provider rallied after reporting strong earnings and growth.

items.headline: The Gap Falls on Weak Old Navy Performance
items.summary: Shares declined after reporting earnings with poor performance by the Old Navy brand, weighing on the full-year outlook.

items.headline: Ambarella Experiences Significant Drop
items.summary: The semiconductor design company's shares fell sharply during today's trading.

items.headline: SentinelOne Drops on Weak Outlook and Layoffs
items.summary: Shares fell after the software company gave a weaker-than-expected second-quarter revenue forecast and announced an 8% reduction in its full-time employees.

items.headline: Technology and Healthcare Lead Sector Performance
items.summary: Technology stocks, particularly those involved in AI, continue their strong ascent, with the S&P 500 Technology sector up nearly 2%. Software stocks are rallying broadly due to positive sentiment. Healthcare is also a top-performing sector in May, showing resilience due to its insulation from interest rate cycles and projected increases in federal spending. Conversely, the Utilities sector has lagged, sliding 4.9% this month amidst concerns over AI data center impacts on electric grids and water supplies.

items.headline: Inflation Remains Elevated; PCE Data Signals 'Fed on Hold' Posture
items.summary: April's PCE inflation increased 0.4%, with core PCE up 0.2%, showing a slight cooling from March but still elevated underlying price pressures. This data, coupled with a downward revision of Q1 GDP to 1.6% from 2.0%, reinforces the Federal Reserve's stance of holding benchmark interest rates steady. Initial jobless claims for the week ended May 23 rose by 5,000 to 215,000, while continuing claims increased by 15,000 to 1,786,000 for the week ended May 16.

items.headline: U.S.-Iran Ceasefire Talks Ease Oil Prices and Geopolitical Risks
items.summary: Progress in U.S.-Iran ceasefire negotiations has positively impacted market sentiment, leading to a decline in crude oil prices. Brent crude fell 1.6% to $92.18 a barrel, and benchmark U.S. crude dropped 0.9% to $88.09 a barrel. This easing of geopolitical risk and lower energy costs are seen as supportive factors for the broader market, potentially shifting investor focus towards cyclicals and consumer discretionary sectors.

items.headline: Strong Corporate Earnings Bolster Market Confidence
items.summary: The latest round of corporate profit reports continues to impress, with companies in the S&P 500 reporting an average profit growth of 28% for the most recent quarter. This strong earnings season has helped to mute persistent inflation worries and is contributing to the market's current rally.

items.headline: Not financial advice
items.summary: This market digest provides general market insights and is not intended as specific investment advice. Investors should conduct their own research and consult with a qualified financial advisor before making any investment decisions.
